message:
fix(security): harden JS extension module resolution with fail-closed empty-roots, canonicalized escape detection, and cross-platform paths

Three security/correctness fixes in resolve_module_path and related
functions:

1. Fail-closed on empty roots: previously, when extension_roots was
   empty, the file:// handler and relative-path handler would skip all
   monotonicity checks and allow resolution of any path. Now empty roots
   immediately returns None, denying resolution when no sandbox boundary
   is configured. This closes a privilege escalation path where an
   extension loaded without explicit roots could read arbitrary files.

2. Monorepo escape detection canonicalization: detect_monorepo_escape
   now calls safe_canonicalize() on each extension root before the
   starts_with comparison. Previously, symlinked roots or roots with
   redundant path components (e.g. /a/../b/) could cause false-positive
   escape detection, incorrectly blocking legitimate module resolution
   within the sandbox.

3. Cross-platform dist-to-src fallback: try_dist_to_src_fallback now
   normalizes backslashes before searching for "/dist/" and uses
   std::path::MAIN_SEPARATOR when constructing the src/ replacement
   path, fixing resolution failures on Windows where paths use
   backslash separators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
